title: Listening - Part 2
description: For each question, choose the correct answer.
instructions: You will hear six short extracts. For questions 8–13, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best according to what you hear.

extracts.context: You hear two people talking about a café.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: Have you tried the café next to the station since it changed owners? || Man: Yes, I went this morning. It looks much brighter now. || Woman: Is the coffee still good? || Man: It is. [8]The best change is that they serve breakfast until midday now[/8]. Before, you had to get there before ten, which never suited me. It should be fine as long as everybody remembers the new arrangement. I am glad we checked the details before making the final decision.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Have you tried the café next to the station since it changed owners?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Yes, I went this morning. It looks much brighter now.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Is the coffee still good?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: It is. The best change is that they serve breakfast until midday now. Before, you had to get there before ten, which never suited me. It should be fine as long as everybody remembers the new arrangement. I am glad we checked the details before making the final decision.

questions.question: What change does the man like best?
questions.explanation: The man says the best change is that breakfast is served until midday.

extracts.context: You hear a woman talking to a friend about moving house.
extracts.audio_script: Man: Are you excited about moving next week? || Woman: Yes, but packing is taking longer than I expected. || Man: Have you hired a van? || Woman: [9]My brother is borrowing one from work, so we don't need to pay for one[/9]. I just need to find more boxes for the kitchen things. That gives us enough time to prepare without changing everything else. It also means we will not have to spend extra money unnecessarily.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Are you excited about moving next week?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Yes, but packing is taking longer than I expected.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Have you hired a van?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: My brother is borrowing one from work, so we don't need to pay for one. I just need to find more boxes for the kitchen things. That gives us enough time to prepare without changing everything else. It also means we will not have to spend extra money unnecessarily.

questions.question: How will the woman move her things?

options.A: She will hire a van herself.
options.B: Her brother will provide a van.
options.C: She will pay a moving company.

questions.explanation: The woman says her brother is borrowing a van from work, so she does not need to pay for one.

extracts.context: You hear two friends talking about a football match.
extracts.audio_script: Boy: Did your team win on Saturday? || Girl: No, we lost two nil, but we played better than last time. || Boy: What went wrong? || Girl: [10]We missed several chances to score in the first half[/10]. After that, the other team got more confident, and it was difficult to come back. I suppose it is a small change, but it makes the situation much easier. That should make the plan easier and help everyone know what to expect.

dialogue.speaker: boy
dialogue.text: Did your team win on Saturday?

dialogue.speaker: girl
dialogue.text: No, we lost two nil, but we played better than last time.

dialogue.speaker: boy
dialogue.text: What went wrong?

dialogue.speaker: girl
dialogue.text: We missed several chances to score in the first half. After that, the other team got more confident, and it was difficult to come back. I suppose it is a small change, but it makes the situation much easier. That should make the plan easier and help everyone know what to expect.

questions.question: Why does the girl think her team lost?

options.C: They failed to score early chances.

questions.explanation: The girl says they missed several chances to score in the first half.

extracts.context: You hear a man speaking at a community centre.
extracts.audio_script: Man: Good evening, everyone. Before the art class starts, I need to mention a small change. || Man: The teacher is here, and the class will finish at the usual time. || Man: However, [11]we are using the room at the end of the corridor tonight because this room has no heating[/11]. Please take your materials with you. I think that is the most sensible choice after looking at the other possibilities. It means there should be fewer problems when the time comes to do it.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Good evening, everyone. Before the art class starts, I need to mention a small change.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: The teacher is here, and the class will finish at the usual time.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: However, we are using the room at the end of the corridor tonight because this room has no heating. Please take your materials with you. I think that is the most sensible choice after looking at the other possibilities. It means there should be fewer problems when the time comes to do it.

questions.question: What has changed about the art class?

options.A: It is in a different room.

questions.explanation: The speaker says they are using another room because the current room has no heating.

extracts.context: You hear two friends discussing a holiday plan.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: Have you booked somewhere to stay in Edinburgh yet? || Man: Not yet. Hotels in the centre are expensive. || Woman: You could stay outside the city and take the bus. || Man: Maybe, but [12]I'd rather stay somewhere central and spend less on restaurants[/12]. I want to be able to walk to the museums. I would rather decide now than leave everything until the last minute. That way, we can enjoy it more and avoid rushing around later.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Have you booked somewhere to stay in Edinburgh yet?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Not yet. Hotels in the centre are expensive.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: You could stay outside the city and take the bus.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Maybe, but I'd rather stay somewhere central and spend less on restaurants. I want to be able to walk to the museums. I would rather decide now than leave everything until the last minute. That way, we can enjoy it more and avoid rushing around later.

questions.question: What does the man prefer?
questions.explanation: The man says he would rather stay somewhere central.

extracts.context: You hear a girl talking about learning to drive.
extracts.audio_script: Man: How are your driving lessons going? || Girl: Better now. At first I was afraid of busy roads. || Man: Did your instructor help? || Girl: Yes. [13]He took me to quiet streets first, so I could practise changing gear without worrying about traffic[/13]. Now I feel much more relaxed. It is not perfect, but it seems better than the other choices we had. The important thing is to keep it simple and not make extra work.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: How are your driving lessons going?

dialogue.speaker: girl
dialogue.text: Better now. At first I was afraid of busy roads.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Did your instructor help?

dialogue.speaker: girl
dialogue.text: Yes. He took me to quiet streets first, so I could practise changing gear without worrying about traffic. Now I feel much more relaxed. It is not perfect, but it seems better than the other choices we had. The important thing is to keep it simple and not make extra work.

questions.question: How did the instructor help the girl?

options.A: He taught her in heavy traffic.
options.C: He let her practise on quiet roads.

questions.explanation: The girl says the instructor took her to quiet streets first so she could practise without worrying about traffic.
